title: xNet is now an open, re-implementable protocol
summary: The data model, sync, identity, and authorization are now a written standard — re-implement xNet in any language, over any database, and interoperate.
highlights: New /docs/protocol section: four normative layers (primitives, data model, replication, authorization) and the xnet/1.0 umbrella version
A language-agnostic conformance corpus with golden vectors and a Python reference kernel that verifies TypeScript-signed changes byte-for-byte
The XPP proposal process for evolving the standard
